Header.value: Is this a relevant case related to worker classification?
Note: Relevant cases contains a court applying the law to the facts and rendering a final decision on the worker's status
Relevant factors - click and highlight text
What is the length of employment
Choose factors on the scale.
(Tri-lateral relationship) Does the worker provide services under an independent service company/entity?
(Managerial Role) Is the worker in a managerial role?
(Commitment) Is there a contract in place stating the employment relationship with the worker?
(Contract) What is the type of contract established with the worker?
(Termination) Is the work relationship terminated after a fixed date or after completion of the set objectives/tasks?
(Minimum Pay or Work) Is the worker guaranteed a minimum amount of work or pay?
(Insurance) Who supplies the occupation liability insurance coverage?

Choice.value: 2 - Both Worker and Principal contributes to liability insurance

Header.value: (Employment Benefits) Is the worker given any employment benefits (Paid time off/Vacation, health and dental benefits, and etc)?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, all benefits normally given to employees
2 - Yes, some benefits normally given to employees

Header.value: (Independence) Can the Worker operate and advertise its own business for the services rendered?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, and the Worker does in practice
2 - Yes, the Worker is capable but DOES NOT in practice

Header.value: (Selection) Is the worker hired for their unique skills, expertise, or talent that distinguishes them from ordinary employees?
(Integration) Is the worker performing activities which are usually necessary or desirable in the usual business or trade of the Hirer?
(Training) Does the Worker require any additional training or education by the Hirer to perform their function?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, the Worker requires substantial training or education necessary for their job
2 - Yes, the Worker requires some training or education ancillary for their job

Header.value: (Remuneration) How is the worker remunerated?

Choice.value: 2 - Salary/Fixed Wages with opportunity for incentives
3 - Mix of salary and paid by commission/per task without incentives
4 - Mix of salary and paid by commission/per task with opportunity for incentives
5 - Paid by Commission/per task only without incentives
6 - Paid by Commission/per task with opportunity for incentives

Header.value: (Payment Procedure) Is the worker required to submit invoices for payment?
(Opportunity for Loss) Is the worker liable for any financial loss?

Choice.value: 2 - Minor risk (pays for some expenses/have some money invested, but not liable for losses to the Hirer's business)
3 - High risk (pays for expenses/have money invested, AND liable for lossess to Hirer's business

Header.value: (Third Party Risk) Is the worker personally liable for damages caused to third parties as a result of carrying on their duties

(Withholdings) Does the Hirer list the worker on payroll and/or withhold taxes and other statutory contributions, including but not limited to, Social Security Systems, Health Insurance, Employment Insurance?
(Supervision) Is the Worker supervised by the Hirer?
(Disciplinary Action) Is the Worker subject to disciplinary action by the Hirer?
(Compliance) Does the Hirer have an obligation to ensure that the Worker complies with local laws?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, the hirer is required to verify that the worker is complying with local laws and regulation
2 - No, the hirer is not required to ensure that the worker complies with local laws
3 - No, the worker can refuse to disclosure these information

Header.value: (Allocation of Work) Which party decides what work is done?

Choice.value: 1 - The Hirer assigns work, and it cannot be turned down by the Worker
2 - The Hirer assigns work, but it can be turned down by the Worker
3 - The Worker decides the work with approval from the Hirer
4 - The Worker decides the work WITHOUT approval

Header.value: (Manner of Work) Which party decides how the work is done?

Choice.value: 1 - The Hirer gives instructions on how to perform the task and the Worker must comply
2 - The Worker decides how to complete the task, but must comply with the requirements set by the Hirer or seek permission from the Hirer
3 - The Worker is free to chose how to complete the task without restrictions

Header.value: (Work Hours) Which party controls the work hours?

Choice.value: 1 - The Hirer sets the work schedule and work hours
2 - The Hirer sets the work hours, but the Worker sets the schedule
3 - The Worker sets the work hours, but the Hirer sets the schedule
4 - The Worker sets their own schedule and work hours

Header.value: (Work Location) Which party decides the work location?

Choice.value: 1 - The Hirer sets the work location
2 - The Worker sets the work location with permission from the Hirer
3 - The Worker sets the work location without permission

Header.value: (Ownership of Resources) Which party supplies the resources needed for the job (i.e. tools, equipment, machines, work premises, and supervision)?

Choice.value: 1 - The Hirer owns/provides all of the resources
2 - The Hirer owns/provides most of the resources, but not all the necessary ones
3 - Both Parties own/provide equal proportions of resources
4 - The Worker owns/provides most of the resources, but not all the necessary ones
5 - The Worker owns/provides all of the resources

Header.value: (Distinction) Is the Worker integrated or held out as part of the hiring organization?

Choice.value: 1 - High Level of Integration (Required to wear uniform, listed on company directories, held out as employee etc.)
2 - Some Level of Integration (Business cards, email addresses, badges etc.)
3 - No Integration (Nothing associating worker as part of business)

Header.value: (Exclusivity) Does the worker take on additional jobs with others?

Choice.value: 1 - No, the worker works exclusively for the Hirer
2 - No, but the worker can others WITH permission from the Hirer
3 - No, but the worker can work for others WITHOUT permission
4 - Yes, the worker works equal amounts with the hirer and others
5 - Yes, the worker does majority of work for others

Header.value: (Delegation) Is the worker able to subcontract work/hire workers?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, with permission from the Principal Hirer

Header.value: (Labor Union) Is the worker associated with a labor union?

Choice.value: 1 - Yes, with permission from the Principal Hirer

Header.value: (Worker Status) What is the worker's status?
